CHARTS / BASKETS
% RETURN, REBASED · EOD 2026-06-05
Rotation ReadCitrini · claude

The tape is screaming concentration, not participation. XLK +10.84% on the month with semis +18.5% and AI infra +25.9% is doing all the heavy lifting, while eight of eleven SPDR sectors printed negative one-month returns. XLC -3.72%, XLU -3.46%, XLY -1.76% — the supposed cyclical (discretionary) is bleeding alongside the defensive (utilities), which tells you this is not a clean risk-on rotation. It is a tech-only melt-up with everything else treading water.

The theme tape confirms it. Cyber +32.8% 1m, AI infra +25.9%, software +24.2%, SaaS +18.9% — every leader sits inside the same semiconductor-and-code complex. Mag 7 only +1.1% means the broader megacap basket is lagging the narrow AI-infra winners; this is a stock-picker's tech rally, not an index trade. Uranium -16%, gold miners -7.6%, fintech -3.8% are the visible casualties of capital being vacuumed into AI compute names.

The stretched-extremes table is the punchline: XLK/SPY z=+3.34 (98th pctile), XLK/XLU z=+3.30, SOXX/SMH z=+3.30, MTUM/SPY z=+3.10, QQQ/TLT z=+2.83. You have four cyclical-over-defensive and factor ratios sitting at three-sigma. Meanwhile XLC/SPY z=-3.46 and MCHI/SPY z=-2.73 are at the opposite extreme. Either tech keeps grinding and these readings get more absurd, or the snapback in laggards is violent and fast.

Narrow AI-infra leadership at 3-sigma vs everything else flat to bleeding

BULL

XLK +10.84% 1m, semis +18.5%, AI infra +25.9% YTD 109.5% — the engine of the cycle is firing and breadth can still catch up if XLF (+1.07%) and energy (+3.0%) keep building.

BEAR

Eight of eleven sectors negative 1m, XLY -1.76%, XLC -3.72%, XLU -3.46% all down together — that is not rotation, that is the rest of the tape getting drained to feed one trade.

TELL

Watch XLK/XLU at z=+3.30: a roll-over here means the tech-funding-defensives trade is unwinding and the broadening trade finally arrives. A fresh high means the squeeze continues and laggards keep bleeding.

⚠ XLK +10.84% with semis +18.5% says cyclical risk-on vs XLY -1.76%, XLC -3.72%, XLU -3.46% all down together says no broad cyclical bid — True risk-on would lift discretionary alongside tech. The fact that XLY is in the bottom three with XLU means tech is being funded by everything else — trust the narrow read, this is concentration, not rotation.
⚠ MTUM/SPY z=+3.10 confirms a momentum regime vs VLUE/SPY z=+2.71 says value is also stretched at the same time — Both factors three-sigma stretched simultaneously is rare and points to the same handful of mega-cap winners straddling both classifications. Trust the momentum read; the value extreme is a labeling artifact of the leaders, not a genuine value bid.

Seven ratios sit at |z|>=2.5 and the tape is lopsided. XLK/SPY +3.34, XLK/XLU +3.30, SOXX/SMH +3.30, MTUM/SPY +3.10 are all the same trade priced four different ways — tech and momentum stretched against everything defensive and broad. With YTD spreads of +27.79% (XLK/XLU) and +22.44% (XLK/XLP), these look like trend, not reversion: the underlying earnings dispersion justifies a lot of it. The mean-reversion candidates are the negative extremes — XLC/SPY z=-3.46 (YTD -12.24%) and MCHI/SPY z=-2.73 — where the pain trade is a bounce in the laggards. XLY/SPY z=-2.58 is the one to watch as a tell on the consumer.
